scope: Concrete next branch after rejecting TileLang late-dKV, detached HBM score pages, and ordinary same-workgroup scorepage widening: keep the Triton 11.13 ms query-major schedule shape, but reimplement the source-boundary owner in HIP/CK/rocWMMA for lower-level dKV epilogue control
required_outputs: optional checksum/debug sinks only for probes

rejected_surfaces.late_dkv_tilelang_or_reducer_only: Does not materialize e2e because PyTorch/Triton still materializes selected K/V, probabilities, scaled-dP, bridge tensors, and calls the faster reducer too late.
rejected_surfaces.two_kernel_p_or_dp_page: Can recompute P/scaled-dP but writes a score/prob/dP slab to HBM before dKV, preserving the memory round trip.
rejected_surfaces.ordinary_same_workgroup_scorepage: Correct S128 and valid S4096 execution, but S4096 mode3 valid dKV is 25.94 ms and mode4 contribution/reduce without dKV writes is 21.77 ms.
rejected_surfaces.wider_same_cta_d_parallel: 512-thread two-D16 no-write mode is 23.65 ms; matching mode6 valid dKV is 24.26 ms, so final dKV writes add only about 0.61 ms in that widened shape. 1024-thread four-D16 mode is 31.36 ms. Widening one CTA does not recover enough D parallelism and shifts the wall into producer/consumer overhead.
rejected_surfaces.cooperative_grid_global_scorepage: HIP cooperative launch works on MI350, but grid sync only enables grid-wide synchronization/global-memory visibility. It does not provide cross-CTA LDS for an on-chip score page.

selected_kernel_contract.owner: CK/rocWMMA/HIP implementation of the Triton-style query-major source-boundary sparse-MLA backward owner
selected_kernel_contract.design_principle: Do not switch to a group-major contribution producer. Preserve the Triton baseline's token/head/query-major selected-window loop because it keeps q and grad_output reuse. Use CK/rocWMMA/HIP only where the lower-level surface matters: MFMA score/contribution tiles and a controllable grouped dKV epilogue before global memory.
schedule: match the Triton baseline decomposition: token tile -> head block -> selected-window/K tile -> D block
query-major token tile owns q/dO reuse
score producer computes QK and dO.V for selected K16/K32 pages
softmax and scaled-dP row terms are produced in on-chip storage
D-parallel consumers immediately consume the score page for dQ and dKV contribution math
group-aware dKV epilogue performs local segmented reduction by compressed group before global writes
no selected K/V, P, grad_logits, or scaled-dP slab is written to HBM

next_code_step: Do not add another standalone dKV reducer first and do not make compressed group the outer contribution loop. Start from the Triton 11.13 ms schedule shape, then build the HIP/CK/rocWMMA source-boundary owner around token/head/query-major selected windows with a CK-style epilogue: reduce selected-slot contributions by compressed group inside the producer tile, emit fewer compact partials or reduced atomics, and only then add dQ. If CK templates become necessary, keep the Python-facing probe separate until the S4096 boundary latency beats 11.13 ms.
